title: The Power of Daily Habits: Small Steps, Big Changes
title_zh: 日常习惯的力量：小步前行，巨大改变
intro_zh: 本文约 1000 词，探讨日常生活中微小习惯如何逐渐改变我们的生活，强调坚持和积累的重要性，适合 CET-4 阅读理解。

paragraphs.en: Have you ever wondered why some people seem to achieve so much while others struggle to make progress? The secret often lies not in grand plans, but in the small, daily habits we build over time. These tiny actions might seem unimportant at first, but their cumulative effect can be truly life-changing.
paragraphs.zh: 你是否曾好奇，为什么有些人看似成就斐然，而另一些人却难以取得进展？秘密通常不在于宏伟的计划，而在于我们日复一日建立的微小日常习惯。这些微不足道的行为起初可能看起来无关紧要，但它们的累积效应可以真正改变生活。

paragraphs.en: Consider the story of James Clear, author of the best-selling book "Atomic Habits." He explains that improving by just 1 percent every day leads to a dramatic change over a year. For instance, reading just ten pages of a book each day amounts to about 3,650 pages a year — that's roughly 12 novels! The key is consistency, not intensity. You don't need to revolutionize your life overnight.
paragraphs.zh: 以畅销书《原子习惯》的作者詹姆斯·克利尔为例。他解释说，每天进步1%，一年后就会带来巨大的变化。例如，每天只读十页书，一年下来大约就是3650页——大约等于12部小说！关键在于持续性，而非强度。你不需要在一夜之间彻底改变你的生活。

paragraphs.en: But how do we build good habits? First, start small. Choose a habit so easy that you can't say no. Want to exercise? Start with one push-up a day. Want to write a book? Write a single sentence. This approach reduces the resistance we often feel when starting something new. Over time, the habit becomes automatic, and you can gradually expand it.
paragraphs.zh: 但我们如何培养好习惯呢？首先，从小处着手。选择一个简单到让你无法拒绝的习惯。想锻炼？从每天一个俯卧撑开始。想写一本书？写一个句子。这种方法减少了我们开始新事物时常常感受到的阻力。随着时间的推移，习惯会变得自动，然后你可以逐渐扩展它。

paragraphs.en: Another strategy is to "stack" new habits onto existing ones. For example, after you brush your teeth every morning (an existing habit), you might do a two-minute meditation. This linking makes it easier to remember and initiate the new behavior. Your brain already knows the routine, so the new habit binds naturally to the old one.
paragraphs.zh: 另一个策略是将新习惯“叠加”到现有习惯上。例如，每天早上刷牙后（一个现有习惯），你可以冥想两分钟。这种关联使得记住和启动新行为更容易。你的大脑已经知道这个流程，所以新习惯自然地与旧习惯结合在一起。

paragraphs.en: Environment also plays a crucial role. If you want to eat healthier, keep fruits and vegetables visible on the counter. If you want to read more, place a book on your pillow. Our surroundings constantly influence our behavior, often without our awareness. By designing a supportive environment, we make good choices easier and bad choices harder.
paragraphs.zh: 环境也起着至关重要的作用。如果你想吃得更健康，就把水果和蔬菜放在台面上显眼的地方。如果你想多读书，就把一本书放在枕头上。我们的环境总是在不知不觉中影响我们的行为。通过设计支持性的环境，我们可以让好的选择更容易，坏的选择更困难。

paragraphs.en: Of course, you will face obstacles along the way. The key is not to be perfect, but to never miss twice. If you skip a day, get back on track the next day. Missing once is an accident; missing twice is the start of a new bad habit. Forgiveness toward yourself and persistence are essential.
paragraphs.zh: 当然，一路上你会遇到障碍。关键不是追求完美，而是绝不错过两次。如果有一天没做，第二天就重新开始。错过一次是意外；错过两次则是新坏习惯的开始。对自己宽容和坚持不懈至关重要。

paragraphs.en: The beauty of habits is that they compound not only in your actions but also in your identity. When you read every day, you become a reader. When you exercise regularly, you become an athlete. This shift in identity is powerful because it reinforces the habit itself. You no longer "have" to do something; you do it because it's who you are.
paragraphs.zh: 习惯的美妙之处在于，它们不仅在行动上累积，也在身份认同上累积。当你每天阅读，你就成为了一个读者。当你定期锻炼，你就成为了一名运动员。这种身份认同的转变非常强大，因为它反过来强化了习惯本身。你不再“必须”做某事；你去做是因为那就是你。

paragraphs.en: In conclusion, never underestimate the power of small habits. Whether it's saving a little money each week, learning a new word daily, or walking an extra five minutes, these seemingly trivial actions add up to remarkable achievements. Start today, and let the compound effect work its magic.
paragraphs.zh: 总之，永远不要低估小习惯的力量。无论是每周存一点钱、每天学一个新单词，还是多走五分钟路，这些看似琐碎的行为会累积成非凡的成就。从今天开始，让复利效应施展它的魔力。
